comments.body:
I think yes, but I would not model it as another normal `next_action` beside `implement | review | fix-pr`.

Today the orchestrator’s action set is deliberately limited: [`AgentAction`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L3) and delegated capabilities only include `implement`, `review`, and `fix-pr` ([capabilities](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/orchestrator-capabilities.ts#L8-L12)). The top-level `/answer` route already exists in the router and posts inline responses ([router answer job](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml#L270-L365)), but the orchestrator currently only dispatches implementation/review/fix workflows or child orchestration ([handoff dispatch](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L1060-L1109)).

My recommendation: add a **terminal clarification path** to the orchestrator, not an automated handoff loop through `answer`.

Concretely:
- Extend planner output with something like `decision: "blocked"` plus `user_message` / `clarification_request`.
- Teach `orchestrate-handoff` to post that message as the visible stop comment instead of the current generic `Sepo orchestration stopped: ...`.
- Keep `answer` out of `AgentAction` unless we also create a real reusable `agent-answer.yml` workflow, because right now answer is a router job, not a dispatchable orchestrator action.
- Keep the chain paused after asking. The user can reply with `/answer`, `/orchestrate`, or `/implement` once they provide the missing context.

That gives the behavior we want: the orchestrator can ask focused questions when it cannot proceed safely, without pretending that “answer” is another autonomous execution step.

Sepo adopted this issue as a sub-orchestrator child of #104.

Stage: terminal-clarification-message
Parent round: 4

<!-- sepo-sub-orchestrator parent:104 stage:terminal-clarification-message state:done parent_round:4 -->
<!-- sepo-sub-orchestrator-adoption -->

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `orchestrate` -> `delegate_issue` (round 4/10).

agent planner selected child issue delegation: Continue the issue #104 sequential child plan. Child #95 finished with SHIP and open PR #112 stacked on #108; #105 is the next concrete orchestrator UX fix and should stack on #112 because it extends the parent stop/blocked comment surface added by #95. Issue #86 is broader and less specified, so leave it for after the clarification/terminal-message path is in place.

**Sepo implementation finished**

- Branch: `agent/implement-issue-105/codex-25268019583`
- Pull request: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/113

Added planner clarification fields for blocked orchestrator decisions, surfaced them as visible terminal stop comments without dispatching `answer`, updated prompt/docs, and verified with `npm run build`, focused node tests, and `git diff --check`.

title: Orchestrator should also include an "answer" route -> such that it can ask users for feedback and clarification?
